title: Avoid per-frame heap allocation
description: Eliminate allocations in the hot loop by pooling and reusing objects so the garbage collector never causes a frame hitch.
body: Use in any per-frame code path on a GC'd or allocation-sensitive runtime. Do not allocate temporary objects, closures, or boxed values every frame, and do not let collections resize each frame. Actions: pre-allocate and reuse buffers, pool frequently spawned objects (bullets, particles), avoid hidden allocations (string concatenation, LINQ/iterator closures), and pre-size collections. Validation signals include a flat allocation graph during steady play and no GC spikes in the frame-time profile. Risk boundary: a GC pause triggered by per-frame garbage produces a visible stutter the player feels; in a real-time loop, hot-path allocation is a performance defect to remove, not defer.
